title: KeyCorp (KEY) Stock Slides as Market Rises: Facts to Know Before You Trade

title: 50 Large-Cap High-Yield Stocks For December
text: Dogsofthedow.com, or DOD, recently published its list of 50 large-cap high-dividend stocks as of 11/27/23 per data from YCharts. The DOD list targeted-investors who “don't want to simply focus on a high dividend yield only to discover that the stock price has plunged or that the corporation went belly-up.”. Investors reduce volatility/risk by limiting high-dividend search to large-well-established companies.

title: Regional Bank KeyCorp Rated Hold For Dividend Growth And Near 7% Yield (Downgrade)
text: KeyCorp has risen 21% since my July strong buy rating, so is due for downgrade to Hold, being less bullish than the current consensus. It remains a strong dividend grower with near 7% dividend yield. Challenges include weak earnings and revenue driven by lower net interest income.

title: KeyCorp (KEY) Surpasses Market Returns: Some Facts Worth Knowing

title: KEYCORP TO PRESENT AT THE GOLDMAN SACHS 2023 U.S. FINANCIAL SERVICES CONFERENCE
text: CLEVELAND , Nov. 20, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) announced today that Chris Gorman, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, and Clark Khayat, Chief Financial Officer, will present at the Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services Conference on Tuesday, December 5, 2023, at 1:40 p.m. ET in New York City.

title: KEYCORP DECLARES QUARTERLY CASH DIVIDEND ON COMMON SHARES AND PREFERRED STOCKS
text: CLEVELAND , Nov. 16, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY) announced today that its Board of Directors declared the following dividends for the fourth quarter of 2023: A cash dividend of $0.205 per share on the corporation's outstanding common shares (NYSE: KEY). The dividend is payable on December 15, 2023, to holders of record of such Common Shares as of the close of business on November 28, 2023; A dividend of $312.50 per share (equivalent to $12.50 per depositary share (CUSIP #493267AK4)) on the corporation's outstanding Fixed-to-Floating Rate Perpetual Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series D (CUSIP #493267603), payable on December 15, 2023 to holders of record as of the close of business on November 30, 2023, for the period commencing on (and including) September 15, 2023 to (but excluding) December 15, 2023; A dividend of $15.3125 per share (equivalent to $.382813 per depositary share (NYSE: KEY.I)) on the corporation's outstanding Fixed-to-Floating Rate Perpetual Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series E (CUSIP #493267801), payable on December 15, 2023 to holders of record as of the close of business on November 30, 2023, for the period commencing on (and including) September 15, 2023 to (but excluding) December 15, 2023; A dividend of $14.1250 per share (equivalent to $.353125 per depositary share (NYSE: KEY.J)) on the corporation's outstanding Fixed Rate Perpetual Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series F (CUSIP #493267884), payable on December 15, 2023 to holders of record as of the close of business on November 30, 2023, for the period commencing on (and including) September 15, 2023 to (but excluding) December 15, 2023; A dividend of $14.0625 per share (equivalent to $.351563 per depositary share (NYSE: KEY.K)) on the corporation's outstanding Fixed Rate Perpetual Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series G (CUSIP #493267850), payable on December 15, 2023 to holders of record as of the close of business on November 30, 2023, for the period commencing on (and including) September 15, 2023 to (but excluding) December 15, 2023; and A dividend of $15.50 per share (equivalent to $.3875 per depositary share (NYSE: KEY.L)) on the corporation's outstanding Fixed Rate Reset Perpetual Non-Cumulative Preferred Stock, Series H (CUSIP #493267835), payable on December 15, 2023 to holders of record as of the close of business on November 30, 2023, for the period commencing on (and including) September 15, 2023 to (but excluding) December 15, 2023.

title: 3 Bill Gross Bank Stocks to Add to Your Buy List
text: Bill Gross, the Bond King, is back in the news this week. Last week, I detailed how Bill Gross focused on equity arbitrage opportunities while forecasting a recession.

title: 3 Blue-Chip Stocks With Strong Dividends
text: As an investor, you need an edge to outperform the market. However, as you reach for performance, you should be cognizant of the risks in the current macro environment.

title: JPMorgan's and PNC's mixed outlook on net interest income draws attention from analyst after industry gathering
text: Big U.S. banks have signaled that they expect net interest income to bottom out in the next few quarters as they keep an eye on whether the cost of consumer deposits will come down, a bank analyst said in a research note on Monday.

title: 7 Dirt-Cheap Bank Stocks with Massive Dividends
text: Bank stocks are down badly this year. Borrowing costs are sky-high, cutting into net interest income.

title: 5 Relatively Secure And Cheap Dividend Stocks To Invest In - November 2023
text: This article is part of our monthly series where we highlight five large-cap, relatively safe, dividend-paying companies offering large discounts to their historical norms. We go over our filtering process to select just five conservative DGI stocks from more than 7,500 companies that are traded on U.S. exchanges, including OTC networks. In addition to the primary list that yields just over 3%, we present two other groups of five DGI stocks each, with the goal of moderate to high yields.

title: KeyCorp's Edge: Capitalizing On Trends For 'Targeted Scale'
text: KeyCorp focuses on high-growth sectors and technology, indicating a solid investment thesis despite income pressures. Valuation suggests KeyCorp may be undervalued, presenting a re-rating opportunity with a substantial dividend yield. Risks include interest rate and regulatory changes, yet KeyCorp's expected return is an attractive 22%.

title: US regional banks shares surge after Bill Gross says they have hit bottom
text: Shares of a handful of U.S. regional banks popped on Thursday after famed bond investor Bill Gross said the danger in investing in them had passed.

title: Albany Black Chamber of Commerce Awarded $200,000 Grant from KeyBank Foundation
text: KeyBank Foundation Impact Grant will provide Albany Black Chamber of Commerce (ABCC) $200,000 to help ABCC build a more vital, sustainable organization to serve its members and the community ALBANY, N.Y. , Nov. 2, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The Albany Black Chamber of Commerce (ABCC) announced an award of $200,000 in grant funding from the KeyBank Foundation.
